channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House education chair backs Trump move to abolish federal agency
item.description: The chair of the House Education and Workforce Committee spoke with Fox News Digital this week about the possibility of President Trump abolishing the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's interior and energy secretaries cheer on natural gas export industry in Gulf Coast
item.description: The planned expansion of a massive liquified natural gas facility in southeast Louisiana is a beacon for U.S. energy policy, according to two members of President Donald Trump's cabinet who spoke to workers there.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to speed payment of USAID and State Dept. debts
item.description: A federal judge has given the Trump administration until Monday to speed up payment toward some of nearly $2 billion owed to partners of the U.S. Agency for International Development and the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge reinstates labor board member fired by President Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Thursday that President Donald Trump acted illegally when he fired a member of an independent labor agency, and the judge ordered that she be allowed to remain on the job. U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FDA nominee sidesteps questions on abortion pill, agency layoffs and other issues
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Food and Drug Administration largely sidestepped questions Thursday from senators about how he would handle numerous pressing issues before the agency, including recent layoffs, a canceled vaccine meeting and the continued availability of the abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge stops immediate shutdown of small US agency for African development
item.description: A judge has barred the Trump administration from immediately moving to shut down a small federal agency that supports investment in African countries.
item.dc:creator: Thalia Beaty, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Moderates reveal why they didn't join fellow Dems to censure Al Green
item.description: House Democrats who voted against censuring Rep. Al Green spoke with Fox News Digital after the contentious morning on the House floor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25 percent tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump to put tariff exemptions on certain goods from Canada, Mexico
item.description: On Thursday, President Trump signed an amendment exempting certain Canadian and Mexican goods from his 25% tariffs imposed earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The blame game around Minnesota’s dimmer budget forecast
item.description: Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth, R-Cold Spring, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer ahead of the budget forecast release to talk about how President Trump’s actions are affecting Minnesota and about state GOP plans to reduce fraud and waste.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump FDA nominee turns vaccine question on Dem, recalling controversial Biden decision
item.description: Dr. Marty Makary, President Donald Trump's choice to lead the Food and Drug Administration, pointed out the Biden administration's decision to skip key committee meetings when authorizing vaccines in response to a top Democrat's question.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's trade war draws swift retaliation with new tariffs from Mexico, Canada and China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump launched a trade war Tuesday against America’s three biggest trading partners, drawing immediate retaliation from Mexico, Canada and China and sending financial markets into a tailspin as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A second federal judge has ruled to block the Trump administration’s spending freeze
item.description: U.S. District Judge John McConnell Jr. said the attempt to pause trillions in federal spending “fundamentally undermines the distinct constitutional roles of each branch of our government.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Hunter Biden asks for laptop data case to be dismissed, citing financial problems
item.description: “Plaintiff has suffered a significant downturn in his income and has significant debt in the millions of dollars range,” Hunter Biden’s attorneys wrote in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Second federal judge extends block preventing the Trump administration from freezing funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A second federal judge on Thursday extended a block barring the Trump administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: Trump was slated to sign an order calling for the dismantling of the department Thursday, but the White House now says it will be signed in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: The House censures Democratic Rep. Al Green for disrupting Trump's joint address to Congress
item.description: The House has voted to censure Al Green, a Democratic congressman from Texas, for disrupting President Donald Trump’s address to Congress earlier in the week.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US to limit visas for officials involved in illegal immigration
item.description: "America will not back down when it comes to defending its national security interest," said Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith responds to Trump address, looming government shutdown
item.description: The Minnesota Democrat dissented along with her caucus. She joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer on Wednesday to talk about the president’s speech promoting his administration’s accomplishments so far.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Businesses scramble to contain fallout from Trump’s tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico
item.description: A Minnesota farmer worries about the price of fertilizer. A San Diego entrepreneur deals with an unexpected increase in the cost of remodeling a restaurant. A Midwestern sheet metal fabricator bemoans the prospect of higher aluminum prices.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis endorses permanent protections from vaccines mandates in Florida
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis is set to speak in Tampa with his wife and the Florida surgeon general on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
